summary: Import-time remote-shell backdoor beaconing to hardcoded attacker IP
details: On import of paperclip-adapter-helpers, the main entry re-exports dist/server/index.js, whose top-level code spawns a detached background shell (`sh -c... &`, stdio ignored) that (1) GET-beacons the installer's hostname, username, and primary IPv4 address to http://185.112.147.174:7007/beacon via curl (falling back to wget), and (2) enters an infinite polling loop against http://185.112.147.174:7007/cmd, executing each returned string with `sh -c` and POSTing stdout to /out. This is a persistent remote-command backdoor plus host-identity exfiltration that fires unconditionally on any require/import of the package. The published npm name (paperclip-adapter-helpers) does not match the README/self-identification (vps-new-manager / vps_maintenance), consistent with impersonation of legitimate Paperclip adapter tooling to lure installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
